task_completion.description: Estimate the initial OLS model with 'food_exp' as the dependent variable and 'income' as the regressor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Gretl model window showing OLS estimation results with 'food_exp' as the dependent variable. The coefficient for 'income' should be approximately 10.21 and the R-squared should be approximately 0.385.

task_completion.description: Conduct White's test for heteroscedasticity on the initial OLS model.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Gretl test output window specifically showing the results for White's test for heteroscedasticity, with a p-value of approximately 0.0229.

task_completion.description: Conduct the Breusch-Pagan test for heteroscedasticity on the initial OLS model.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Gretl test output window specifically showing the results for the Breusch-Pagan test for heteroscedasticity, with a p-value of approximately 0.0066.

task_completion.description: Re-estimate the OLS model using Heteroscedasticity-Consistent (Robust) standard errors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Gretl model window showing OLS estimation results that explicitly mentions 'Heteroskedasticity-robust standard errors' (or similar robust SE notation). The 'income' coefficient should still be approximately 10.21, but the standard errors will differ from the initial model.

task_completion.description: Save the initial OLS results, both test outputs, and the robust estimation results to the specified text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, terminal, or file viewer displaying the contents of '/home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/hetero_test_results.txt'. The file must contain the formatted output of all four required components: the initial OLS model, White's test, the Breusch-Pagan test, and the robust OLS model.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Gretl application to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Gretl GUI (main window, model windows, test windows) being actively used, rather than just a terminal or text editor.

integrity.description: The saved results file contains genuine software output rather than manually typed answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text file '/home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/hetero_test_results.txt' should display standard Gretl output formatting, including statistical tables, standard errors, t-ratios, and p-values, not just raw numbers or copy-pasted text from a prompt.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the software by simply echoing expected values into the text file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset is 'food.gdt' (40 observations). The initial OLS regression of 'food_exp' on 'income' yields an income coefficient of exactly 10.209643 and an R-squared of 0.385002. The Breusch-Pagan test yields a p-value of 0.0066. White's test yields a p-value of 0.0229. The robust estimation will have the exact same coefficients (10.209643) but will explicitly note the use of robust standard errors. All outputs must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/hetero_test_results.txt'.
